title: Cylicodaphne garciae
intro: Cylicodaphne garciae is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae, native to the Philippines. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cylicodaphne garciae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cylicodaphne garciae include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Cylicodaphne garciae is classified as a species of plant within the family Lauraceae. This evergreen shrub or small tree is native to the Philippines. It belongs to the genus Cylicodaphne, which is a group of flowering plants known for their aromatic leaves. The classification of Cylicodaphne garciae is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the clade Angiosperms and the clade Magnoliids. The plant's classification is essential for its identification and conservation.
